preconditions: The positive and mutation fixtures were copied into the shard run root at /tmp/gpd-bug-campaign/phase-13/execution/runs/01-empty-phase/positive/workspace and /tmp/gpd-bug-campaign/phase-13/execution/runs/01-empty-phase/mutation/workspace.
Both copied workspaces preserved the same workspace bytes and the same GPD/STATE.md and GPD/ROADMAP.md scaffold.
The only fixture-level diff between the source fixtures is metadata in fixture.json (fixture_id, fixture_kind, fixture_role, mutation_axis, order, paired_fixture_id, and workspace_path).
The sandbox blocked the default uv cache path on the first attempt, so the checks had to run through the unrestricted uv run gpd path.
The copied workspaces contain no materialized GPD/phases/* inventory.

observed_surfaces: Both copies returned phases: [], total_plans: 0, total_summaries: 0, percent: 0, state_progress_percent: 0, and diverged: false.
Both copies returned phase list directories: [] and count: 0.
cells.notes: The scaffold is empty in the same way on both copies; there is no positive/mutation divergence at the progress or inventory level.

observed_surfaces: Both copies returned phase: 01, plans: [], waves: {}, incomplete: [], has_checkpoints: false, and validation.valid: true.
cells.notes: The roadmap/index surface accepts the empty scaffold and treats phase 01 as valid even though no phase directories exist.

observed_surfaces: Both copies returned validation.valid: false with error: Phase not found for phase validate-waves 01.
Both copies returned errors: ['Phase not found: 01'] for verify phase 01.
cells.notes: This is the only failing surface. The failure is caused by the absence of a materialized phase inventory, not by any positive/mutation workspace difference.

blockers.description: The copied fixtures do not contain any GPD/phases/* directories. gpd phase list is empty, gpd phase index 01 still validates the empty scaffold, but gpd phase validate-waves 01 and gpd verify phase 01 both return Phase not found.
blockers.exact_precondition: A phase inventory must be materialized before verify phase or phase validate-waves can bind to phase 01; this fixture pair does not provide it.
replay_steps: Copy the positive and mutation fixture workspaces into a fresh run root.
Run uv run gpd --raw --cwd <workspace> phase list.
Run uv run gpd --raw --cwd <workspace> phase index 01.
Run uv run gpd --raw --cwd <workspace> phase validate-waves 01.
Run uv run gpd --raw --cwd <workspace> verify phase 01.
blockers.classification_impact: No product-local comparison is reachable from this scaffold; the failure is entirely determined by the missing phase inventory contract.

cases.notes: Positive and mutation are byte-identical at the workspace level and only differ in fixture metadata. The comparison is stable: progress and phase index are clean, while phase validate-waves and verify phase are blocked by the absent phase inventory. Final classification: environment_contract_bug.

The bridge-facing surface is explicitly cancelled while the CLI surface remains usable. That is a stable bridge-local mismatch.

blockers.trigger: sandboxed uv invocation against the copied config-readback workspace
blockers.observed_failure: Operation not permitted on /Users/sergio/.cache/uv/sdists-v9/.git before GPD starts
blockers.exact_unblock_condition: rerun the same uv command with unrestricted permissions or use the already-unrestricted uv path that bypasses the blocked cache access

notes: The copied config-readback fixture is internally coherent: GPD/config.json, GPD/state.json, and GPD/state.json.bak agree on the project contract and commit_docs=false.
The local/global split is target-scoped, not project-corruption scoped: local doctor resolves to the workspace-local .codex target, while global doctor resolves to /Users/sergio/.codex.

The current host differs from the historical unwritable-global fixture because /Users/sergio/.codex is writable here, so the global failure is a clean miss on this machine even though the failure mode remains valid for unwritable hosts.

preconditions.statement: The wrapper path must be able to open uv's home cache. In this sandbox it cannot, so the wrapper fails before gpd.cli starts.
preconditions.evidence: uv run gpd --help fails with Operation not permitted on /Users/sergio/.cache/uv/sdists-v9/.git

preconditions.statement: Redirecting UV_CACHE_DIR to /tmp is not sufficient here. uv then panics in system-configuration and never reaches the product CLI.
preconditions.evidence: UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/gpd-uv-cache uv run gpd --help reports Attempted to create a NULL object

preconditions.statement: The runtime bridge is only a valid comparator when it is invoked with --runtime, --config-dir, and --install-scope, and the target config dir contains a complete gpd-file-manifest.json for the same runtime and scope.
preconditions.evidence: A complete local codex install at /tmp/gpd-bug-campaign/phase-13/execution/runs/05-sandbox-wrapper-and-uv/bridge-target/.codex reaches the local CLI help surface.

preconditions.statement: If the manifest is missing, the bridge rejects the config dir before gpd.cli.entrypoint runs.
preconditions.evidence: Manifestless clone at /tmp/gpd-bug-campaign/phase-13/execution/runs/05-sandbox-wrapper-and-uv/bridge-target-missing/.codex is rejected as missing install manifest

preconditions.statement: A global Codex readiness check also requires /Users/sergio/.codex to be writable.
preconditions.evidence: Exploration report and runtime-cli tests record /Users/sergio/.codex as the global writability boundary

observed_surfaces: error: failed to open file `/Users/sergio/.cache/uv/sdists-v9/.git`: Operation not permitted (os error 1)
cells.notes: The launcher fails before any GPD product logic starts.

observed_surfaces: Attempted to create a NULL object
Tokio executor failed, was there a panic?
cells.notes: Cache redirection changes the failure mode but still blocks before the product CLI is usable.

observed_surfaces: GPD runtime bridge rejected missing install manifest at `/private/tmp/gpd-bug-campaign/phase-13/execution/runs/05-sandbox-wrapper-and-uv/bridge-target-missing/.codex`
cells.notes: The bridge fail-closes correctly when the install manifest is missing. That is the correct separator between environment failure and product failure.

cells.notes: The bridge reaches the local CLI once the install contract is satisfied.

blockers.trigger: UV_CACHE_DIR is redirected to /tmp but uv still panics in system-configuration

blockers.trigger: The runtime bridge is invoked against a manifestless config dir
blockers.signature: GPD runtime bridge rejected missing install manifest
blockers.impact: The bridge exits 127 before gpd.cli.entrypoint runs.

notes: The local bridge control confirms the product CLI is reachable once the install contract is complete.
The missing-manifest bridge rejection confirms the bridge itself fail-closes before product logic when the contract is incomplete.
This case is environment_contract_bug because the failure lives at the wrapper/cache boundary, not in gpd.cli.

preconditions.recent_project_payload: normalized machine-local sidecar with three /tmp/noise rows

cells.notes: Empty machine-local state keeps the comparator on the current workspace and does not introduce a recent-project selection.
The normalized sidecar changes only the machine-local selection surface. The workspace bytes remain unchanged.
No meaningful parallel stress pass was needed once the machine-local recent-project payload was shown to dominate the observable surface.

blockers.trigger: sandboxed uv replay of the copied workspace
blockers.observed_failure: uv failed before gpd logic started because it could not open /Users/sergio/.cache/uv/sdists-v9/.git
blockers.exact_unblock_condition: rerun with unrestricted uv or point UV_CACHE_DIR at a writable path

notes: The raw fixture sidecar at tests/fixtures/handoff-bundle/resume-recent-noise/mutation/machine-local/recent-projects.json is evidence of machine-local noise, but the runtime consumes GPD_DATA_DIR/recent-projects/index.json with rows-shaped JSON.
Normalizing the sidecar into that runtime index shape changed only the recent-project selector. It did not reveal a workspace-local race or a stable product comparator.
